channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: After D.C.'s Reflecting Pool gets repainted, visitors ask: What changed?
item.description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.
item.content:encoded:
The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Nick Jonas steals Paul Rudd's 'Power Ballad' in a profound story about art and honesty
item.description: In 'Power Ballad,' a wedding singer played by Paul Rudd writes a hit — and a popstar makes it his.
item.content:encoded:
In 'Power Ballad,' a wedding singer played by Paul Rudd writes a hit — and a popstar makes it his.

(Image credit: David Cleary)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: 2 young directors strike big at the box office with 'Backrooms' and 'Obsession'
item.description: Backrooms, by 20-year-old filmmaker Kane Parsons, is set in a mysterious maze of abandoned offices. Curry Barker, 26, tells a horror story about consent and male loneliness in Obsession.
item.content:encoded: Backrooms, by 20-year-old filmmaker Kane Parsons, is set in a mysterious maze of abandoned offices. Curry Barker, 26, tells a horror story about consent and male loneliness in Obsession.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Angela Lansbury reflects on her great performances on stage and screen
item.description: The Tony Award-winning actor, who died in 2022, starred in the Broadway musicals Mame, Gypsy and Sweeney Todd, as well as in the TV series Murder, She Wrote. Originally broadcast in 1980.
item.content:encoded: The Tony Award-winning actor, who died in 2022, starred in the Broadway musicals Mame, Gypsy and Sweeney Todd, as well as in the TV series Murder, She Wrote. Originally broadcast in 1980.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Tony winner Alan Cumming looks back on life in 'Cabaret'
item.description: Cumming has starred in the musical Cabaret three times. He talks about everything from his costume (which he calls a "Wonder Bra" for men) to the show's darker themes. Originally broadcast in 2014.
item.content:encoded: Cumming has starred in the musical Cabaret three times. He talks about everything from his costume (which he calls a "Wonder Bra" for men) to the show's darker themes. Originally broadcast in 2014.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: China's highest bridge brings tourists and internet access to remote communities
item.description: A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.
item.content:encoded:
A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: NPR photojournalist David Gilkey, in remembrance
item.description: David Gilkey, an NPR photojournalist who documented tragedy and hope, was killed in Afghanistan in 2016 along with NPR's Afghan interpreter and fellow journalist Zabihullah Tamanna.
item.content:encoded: David Gilkey, an NPR photojournalist who documented tragedy and hope, was killed in Afghanistan in 2016 along with NPR's Afghan interpreter and fellow journalist Zabihullah Tamanna.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A global World Cup tradition catches fire in the U.S.: Panini sticker collecting
item.description: Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.
item.content:encoded:
Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Earth, Wind & Fire documentary explores band's creative genius
item.description: A new HBO documentary by Questlove tells the story of the R&B band Earth, Wind & Fire. Morning Edition host A Martinez speaks with band members Philip Bailey, Verdine White and Ralph Johnson.
item.content:encoded:
Morning Edition about the new HBO documentary Earth, Wind & Fire (To Be Celestial vs That's the Weight of the World)'/>

A new HBO documentary by Questlove tells the story of the R&B band Earth, Wind & Fire. Morning Edition host A Martinez speaks with band members Philip Bailey, Verdine White and Ralph Johnson.

(Image credit: Erika Aguilar)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: 'The Wire' actor Clarke Peters explains why he couldn't say no to 'The Boroughs'
item.description: "He's the guy I want to be when I grow up," Peters says of his Wire character, Lester Freamon. In The Boroughs, Peters plays a member of a retirement community that's plagued by mysterious forces.
item.content:encoded: "He's the guy I want to be when I grow up," Peters says of his Wire character, Lester Freamon. In The Boroughs, Peters plays a member of a retirement community that's plagued by mysterious forces.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
